title: Occupational therapists
description: Assess, treat, and care for patients by manipulation of spine and musculoskeletal system. May provide spinal adjustment or address sacral or pelvic misalignment. Diagnose and treat diseases and deformities of the human foot. Assess, plan, and organize rehabilitative programs that help build or restore vocational, homemaking, and daily living skills, as well as general independence, to persons with disabilities or developmental delays. Use therapeutic techniques, adapt the individual's environment, teach skills, and modify specific tasks that present barriers to the individual. (Based on 13 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Complete and maintain necessary records.
Communicate client assessment findings and recommendations in oral, written, audio, video, or other forms.
Confer with other professionals on client's treatment team to develop, coordinate, or integrate treatment plans.
Assess clients' functioning in areas such as vision, orientation and mobility skills, social and emotional issues, cognition, physical abilities, and personal goals.
Evaluate patients' progress and prepare reports that detail progress.
Develop individualized treatment plans based on assessments, patient interests, and therapy goals.
Conduct therapy sessions to improve clients' mental and physical well-being using various activities.
Instruct patients in activities and techniques designed to meet their specific physical or psychological needs.
Plan, organize, and conduct occupational therapy programs in various settings to help rehabilitate persons with disabilities.
Train clients to use adaptive equipment and mobility devices to enhance independence and functioning.
Educate patients on topics such as ergonomics, exercise, nutrition, and the healing process to promote well-being and independence.
Maintain detailed records of health care plans, patient histories, and treatment reports for effective care management.
Assess patients' general physical appearance and collect health histories to make accurate diagnoses and treatment decisions.
Develop individual treatment plans and strategies for patients, considering their unique needs and conditions.
Monitor treatment outcomes and recommend alterations to enhance health restoration and maintenance.
Collaborate with other health professionals to ensure comprehensive patient care and optimal treatment paths.
Maintain professional development through continuing education and training opportunities in therapeutic techniques.
Perform mobilizations and adjustments using principles of massage and resistance to alleviate musculoskeletal issues.
Follow standard quality, safety, environmental, and infection control policies in therapy practices.
Interpret clinical tests and diagnostic results to inform treatment plans and interventions.
